nodes.context: Powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, Italy, with strong ramifications throughout Italy.
Criminal and mafia-type organisation in Calabria, Italy.
Old 'Ndrangheta clan of Reggio Calabria, involved in tobacco smuggling.
'Ndrangheta clan that supported De Stefano in the first war and later became rival.
'Ndrangheta clan that supported De Stefano against Tripodo.
Camorra organization led by Raffaele Cutolo, allied with De Stefano in drug trafficking.
'Ndrangheta clan, underbosses of De Stefano, later rival in second war.
Neighbouring 'ndrina in Villa San Giovanni, rival in second war.
Powerful 'Ndrangheta clan allied with De Stefano through marriage.
Sicilian mafia organization with joint ventures with De Stefano.
Neapolitan mafia organization with joint ventures with De Stefano.
Roman criminal organization with contacts to De Stefano.
'Ndrangheta clan allied with De Stefano in second war.
'Ndrangheta clan allied with De Stefano in second war.
Traditionalist 'Ndrangheta chief opposed to La Santa, killed in first war.

edges.evidence_quote: The De Stefano 'ndrina ... is one of the most powerful clans of the 'Ndrangheta
edges.context: De Stefano is a clan within the 'Ndrangheta organization.

edges.evidence_quote: The De Stefano brothers ... would come to prominence as members of the 'Ndrangheta clan of Domenico Tripodo
edges.context: De Stefano brothers were originally members of Tripodo's clan.

edges.evidence_quote: The war started with an attack on the brothers Giorgio, Paolo and Giovanni De Stefano ... ordered by Tripodo.
edges.context: Tripodo attacked De Stefano, starting the first 'Ndrangheta war.

edges.evidence_quote: the De Stefanos won a monopoly ... with the support of the Piromalli and Mammoliti 'ndrine
edges.context: Piromalli supported De Stefano against Tripodo.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1977 disagreements about business interests emerged between Piromalli and the De Stefano clan. A hit squad headed by Giuseppe 'Peppe' Piromalli killed Giorgio De Stefano.
edges.context: Piromalli killed De Stefano boss Giorgio, starting conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: the De Stefanos won a monopoly ... with the support of the Piromalli and Mammoliti 'ndrine
edges.context: Mammoliti supported De Stefano against Tripodo.

edges.evidence_quote: Tripodo was killed with the help of Camorra boss Raffaele Cutolo , the boss of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata (NCO) who worked with the De Stefano’s in drug trafficking.
edges.context: NCO cooperated with De Stefano in drug trafficking and helped kill Tripodo.

edges.evidence_quote: A second 'Ndrangheta war was triggered by the marriage between Giuseppina Condello – the sister of the Condello brothers, underbosses of De Stefano – and Antonio Imerti
edges.context: Condello were underbosses of De Stefano before the war.

edges.evidence_quote: The bloody six-year war between the Condello -Imerti clan and De Stefano allied with the Tegano clan caused 621 deaths.
edges.context: De Stefano fought against Condello-Imerti alliance in second war.

edges.evidence_quote: The bloody six-year war between the Condello -Imerti clan and De Stefano allied with the Tegano clan caused 621 deaths.
edges.context: De Stefano fought against Condello-Imerti alliance in second war.

edges.evidence_quote: the wedding sealed the alliance between the De Stefanos and the powerful Tegano clan.
edges.context: De Stefano allied with Tegano through marriage during second war.

edges.evidence_quote: the De Stefanos were the representation of the manager-criminal controlling a crime multinational with joint ventures with ... Nitto Santapaola and Francesco Ferrera from Cosa Nostra in Catania
edges.context: De Stefano had joint ventures with Cosa Nostra members.

edges.evidence_quote: the De Stefanos were the representation of the manager-criminal controlling a crime multinational with joint ventures with Raffaele Cutolo from the Camorra
edges.context: De Stefano had joint ventures with Camorra boss Cutolo.

edges.evidence_quote: From his law office in Rome, he also had contacts with the Banda della Magliana.
edges.context: De Stefano member Giorgio had contacts with Banda della Magliana.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Second 'Ndrangheta war (1985-1991): Libri 'ndrina and Latella 'ndrina
edges.context: Libri 'ndrina was allied with De Stefano in the second war.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Second 'Ndrangheta war (1985-1991): Libri 'ndrina and Latella 'ndrina
edges.context: Latella 'ndrina was allied with De Stefano in the second war.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Second 'Ndrangheta war (1985-1991): Condello , Imerti , Serraino 'ndrina and Rosmini 'ndrina
edges.context: Serraino 'ndrina was a rival in the second war.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Second 'Ndrangheta war (1985-1991): Condello , Imerti , Serraino 'ndrina and Rosmini 'ndrina
edges.context: Rosmini 'ndrina was a rival in the second war.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: First 'Ndrangheta war (1974-76): Domenico Tripodo and Antonio Macrì
edges.context: Macrì was a rival in the first war.
